title: Volodymyr Zelensky's exclusive interview with Le Monde on the war in Ukraine
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president shares his perspective on a possible end to the conflict and urges the US and all allied nations to maintain their support for Ukraine.

title: 'As he approached the sands of the Arabian-Persian Gulf, Trump fell prey to a common phenomenon in these lands: mirages'
G7 foreign ministers demand 'immediate' end to attacks against civilians in Middle East war
ICC member states divided over prosecutor Karim Khan's fate
subhead: A report submitted by three judges concluded there was 'insufficient evidence' to establish misconduct by Karim Khan. However, member states remain divided on what steps to take next.

title: El-Daein Hospital in Darfur was deliberately struck by Sudanese army
subhead: The drone attack on the healthcare facility on March 20 killed 70 people, including about 15 children. Initially described as a mistake, the strike targeted paramilitaries from the Rapid Support Forces, who had turned the building into a command and medical center.

title: Cybercrime, the blind spot in museum security
subhead: Dozens of online ticketing systems in France's cultural sector were shut down following a large-scale cyberattack targeting Vivaticket, the leading operator in the field, on March 2. French institutions are still reviewing their defense systems.

title: Israeli writer Etgar Keret wonders if life 'must be reduced to passively reacting to the orders of warlords'
subhead: 'Writers on the war in the Middle East' 4/4. Le Monde asked Sinan Antoon (Iraq), Etgar Keret (Israel) and Diane Mazloum (France, Lebanon) for their perspectives on the war.
byline: Etgar Keret,  Diane Mazloum and  Sinan Antoon

Volodymyr Zelensky: 'The pressure on Russia is insufficient. Not only from the US, but also from Europe'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president expressed concern about the consequences of the war in the Middle East on the conflict between Kyiv and Moscow. Pressured by both the Americans and the Russians to give up the Donbas, he defended the region's strategic importance and warned Europeans of the urgent need to lift the Hungarian veto on the €90 billion loan approved in 2025.

title: The US and Israel are fighting different wars against the same foe
subhead: Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu share many objectives. However, their respective public opinions and electoral interests may ultimately highlight significant differences between the two leaders.

title: 'Trump would benefit from rereading Obama's 2015 speech on Iran'
subhead: The US president belatedly realized that the military option carried significant risks. He did not rely on his administration's recognized experts on Iran, notes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.
